topic: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
researcher_bg: An HCI researcher focused on medical education technology who seeks to understand presentation workflows and skill acquisition in structured terms but has no clinical training background.
expert_bg: A senior clinician and clinical educator who has delivered and evaluated hundreds of oral case presentations, and relies on deeply internalized judgment to organize, prioritize, and adapt case narratives for different clinical audiences.

q_01__shared_root.label: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
q_01__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

q_01__exp_L1_00.label: Expert Clinical Presenter
q_01__exp_L1_00.description: Senior clinician perspective on organizing, prioritizing, and adapting case narratives for different clinical audiences.

q_01__exp_L2_00_00.label: Cognitive Synthesis & Story Building
q_01__exp_L2_00_00.description: How raw patient data is mentally processed into a coherent clinical narrative before delivery.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Scanning for the Clinical Story
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: Skimming the chart for red flags and the diagnostic puzzle rather than reading front-to-back.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Instinctive Outlier Detection
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: Recognizing values that don't fit the patient's baseline without needing to check references.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_02.label: Crafting the Summary One-Liner
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_02.description: Condensing the patient's identity and main problem into a single opening sentence.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_03.label: Prioritizing by Acuity & Educational Value
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_03.description: Ordering problems by life-threat level, but also by what the audience needs to learn.

q_01__exp_L2_00_01.label: Audience & Context Calibration
q_01__exp_L2_00_01.description: Adjusting content and delivery based on who is listening and the constraints of the clinical setting.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Tailoring to Role & Expertise
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: Shifting focus from reasoning/differential for trainees to management decisions for seniors.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Simplifying for Multidisciplinary Teams
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: Adjusting jargon and context when nurses or pharmacists are present to ensure alignment.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_02.label: Adapting to Venue & Time Constraints
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_02.description: Balancing brevity for workflow rounds against depth for teaching conferences.

q_01__exp_L2_00_02.label: Delivery & Defense Mechanisms
q_01__exp_L2_00_02.description: Managing the live interaction, handling uncertainty, and defending clinical decisions during Q&A.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_00.label: Navigating Uncertainty & Honesty
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_00.description: Admitting gaps in knowledge while offering a reasoned hypothesis instead of guessing.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_01.label: Defending Without Arguing
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_01.description: Standing firm on the plan without becoming defensive when challenged by seniors.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_02.label: Real-Time Narrative Adjustment
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_02.description: Shifting emphasis or depth mid-presentation based on audience engagement cues.

q_01__shared_root.label: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
q_01__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

q_01__res_L1_00.label: Cognitive Workflow & Information Foraging
q_01__res_L1_00.description: Examining the internal mental processes and strategies used to extract, filter, and organize patient data before speaking.

q_01__res_L2_00_00.label: Data Triage & Filtering
q_01__res_L2_00_00.description: Understanding how experts decide what information is signal versus noise during chart review.

q_01__res_leaf_00_00_00.label: Identify heuristics for data relevance
q_01__res_leaf_00_00_00.description: Determine the specific rules or intuition experts use to filter chart data into 'relevant' versus 'irrelevant' buckets before presentation.

q_01__res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Resolve conflicting data points
q_01__res_leaf_00_00_01.description: Explore how contradictory information in the patient record is reconciled during the review phase to ensure narrative consistency.

q_01__res_L2_00_01.label: Narrative Synthesis
q_01__res_L2_00_01.description: Investigating how raw data is transformed into a coherent linear story structure.

q_01__res_leaf_00_01_00.label: Map non-linear to linear conversion
q_01__res_leaf_00_01_00.description: Document the steps taken to convert non-linear chart entries into a linear chronological story for the audience.

q_01__res_leaf_00_01_01.label: Prioritize assessment over history
q_01__res_leaf_00_01_01.description: Understand how the 'assessment and plan' section is weighted against the 'history' during the preparation phase.

q_01__res_L1_01.label: Audience Modeling & Contextual Framing
q_01__res_L1_01.description: Exploring how the presenter adapts the content and structure based on the listener and the physical environment.

q_01__res_L2_01_00.label: Listener Profiling
q_01__res_L2_01_00.description: Analyzing how the presenter mentalizes the audience to tailor communication.

q_01__res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Estimate audience baseline knowledge
q_01__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: Investigate how the presenter estimates the audience's prior knowledge to adjust medical terminology and depth.

q_01__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Customize content by specialty
q_01__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Identify what specific clinical details are added or omitted based on the listener's specific role or specialty.

q_01__res_L2_01_01.label: Environmental Constraints
q_01__res_L2_01_01.description: Understanding how physical and temporal constraints shape the preparation strategy.

q_01__res_leaf_01_01_00.label: Adapt prep to physical setting
q_01__res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Analyze how the physical setting (e.g., hallway rounds vs. conference room) dictates the length and detail of preparation.

q_01__res_leaf_01_01_01.label: Plan for anticipated time limits
q_01__res_leaf_01_01_01.description: Understand how anticipated time constraints shape the depth of the initial chart review and scripting.

q_01__res_L1_02.label: Temporal & Artifact Interaction
q_01__res_L1_02.description: Focusing on the timing of preparation phases and the use of external tools to support memory.

q_01__res_L2_02_00.label: Preparation Phasing & Externalization
q_01__res_L2_02_00.description: Examining the timeline of prep and the creation of supporting materials.

q_01__res_leaf_02_00_00.label: Offload memory via artifacts
q_01__res_leaf_02_00_00.description: Identify what physical or digital notes are created during prep to reduce cognitive load during delivery.

q_01__res_leaf_02_00_01.label: Define workflow transition points
q_01__res_leaf_02_00_01.description: Determine the specific moment where information gathering stops and narrative rehearsal begins.

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Finding a contradiction in a friend's story

structural_mapping.logic: Recognizing mismatch without external verification
structural_mapping.outputs: Identifying hidden truth rather than correcting error

analogy.explanation: The researcher treats conflicting data as noise to be fixed for consistency, but the expert treats it as signal. Like finding a contradiction in a friend's story, the mismatch isn't an error to edit out; it is the clue that reveals the true situation.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you find conflicting labs in a chart, do you smooth them out to make the narrative consistent, or do you keep the tension because that contradiction is actually the diagnosis?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Prioritizing emails by importance over arrival time

structural_mapping.logic: Filter by diagnostic value rather than timestamp

analogy.explanation: The researcher assumes you organize data by when it arrived (chronological), but like sorting emails by importance, you reorder data by diagnostic value, meaning the 'story' is structured by criticality, not chronology.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you build your presentation, do you ever rearrange the timeline so the critical diagnosis comes first, even if that information appeared last in the patient's chart?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Spotting a typo while proofreading

structural_mapping.logic: Scan for specific errors or red flags anywhere vs. reading sections linearly

analogy.explanation: Both involve filtering for value. Like spotting typos, the expert scans for signals anywhere, revealing the researcher's 'section weighting' model mismatches the expert's signal-based navigation.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you're prepping a complex case, do you find yourself weighing the history against the assessment, or do you just hunt for the red flags wherever they hide?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

probes.question: Which information source do you consult first?
When data conflicts with observation, what takes precedence?
How do you mentally group patient data?
Which change pattern triggers immediate attention?
What contradiction suggests a data error?
Whose report weighs most heavily?
What time window defines an urgent change?
What visual cue signals an outlier?

hypothetical_scenarios.scenario: If access to Historical Records changed from 'available' to 'unavailable', would your judgment about 'Instinctive Outlier Detection' change?
If the Patient appearance changed from 'stable' to 'distressed', would your judgment about 'Instinctive Outlier Detection' change?
If the data organization changed from 'Organ System' to 'Timeline', would your judgment about 'Instinctive Outlier Detection' change?
If the trend pattern changed from 'Slow Drift' to 'Sudden Spike', would your judgment about 'Instinctive Outlier Detection' change?
If the Vital Sign Mismatch changed from 'present' to 'absent', would your judgment about 'Instinctive Outlier Detection' change?
If the Patient Report changed from 'consistent' to 'contradictory', would your judgment about 'Instinctive Outlier Detection' change?
If the rate of change changed from 'Hours' to 'Seconds', would your judgment about 'Instinctive Outlier Detection' change?
If the display format changed from 'Trend Line Angle' to 'Numeric Highlight', would your judgment about 'Instinctive Outlier Detection' change?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Spotting a typo in your own name

structural_mapping.inputs: Visual patient data stream vs. Written text of a familiar name
structural_mapping.logic: Match against internalized baseline vs. Match against memory of correct spelling
structural_mapping.outputs: Instant anomaly flag vs. Instant error recognition

analogy.explanation: This analogy clarifies that the expert describes the automatic alert (seeing the typo), while the researcher's concept describes the subsequent investigation to determine the correct value, distinguishing immediate detection from deliberate resolution.

assistance_payload.scenario: When a number instantly feels wrong to you, is that gut feeling separate from the moment you decide which record to trust, or do you experience them as one step?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Writing a newspaper headline

structural_mapping.inputs: Full patient chart / Entire news article
structural_mapping.logic: Synthesize main story angle / Distill core message

analogy.explanation: Both processes reduce large inputs into a single output, but like writing a headline, the expert's logic prioritizes narrative cohesion over simple data exclusion, showing relevance depends on the story angle.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you craft that opening sentence, do you start by crossing out normal lab values, or do you find the patient's story first and only keep data that drives that narrative?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

probes.question: When defining the patient, which identifier takes precedence:
Is the problem description driven primarily by:
Do you adjust detail based primarily on:
Do you choose words based primarily on:
Does the setting dictate prioritizing:
Do you state the problem as:
Is the one-liner aimed primarily at:
Do you include social factors when:

hypothetical_scenarios.scenario: If the patient changes from '55M' to 'CEO', would your judgment about 'Crafting the Summary One-Liner' change?
If the problem shifts from 'chest pain' to 'stable angina', would your judgment about 'Crafting the Summary One-Liner' change?
If the listener changes from a Cardiologist to a General Internist, would your judgment about 'Crafting the Summary One-Liner' change?
If you replace 'MI' with 'Heart Attack', would your judgment about 'Crafting the Summary One-Liner' change?
If the handoff occurs at 3 AM vs. during rounds, would your judgment about 'Crafting the Summary One-Liner' change?
If the diagnosis goes from 'confirmed PE' to 'possible PE', would your judgment about 'Crafting the Summary One-Liner' change?
If the goal changes from 'sign-out' to 'consult request', would your judgment about 'Crafting the Summary One-Liner' change?
If the patient is homeless vs. housed, would your judgment about 'Crafting the Summary One-Liner' change?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

assistance_payload.validate_focus: The expert emphasizes that crafting the summary one-liner requires condensing the patient's identity and main problem into a single, consistent opening sentence. This focus ensures the clinical narrative remains accurate regardless of external variables.

pivot.limitation: Staying only on the expert's concern misses how the researcher's focus on physical settings dictates preparation length and detail.
pivot.research_goal: The researcher is trying to understand how environmental constraints shape the preparation strategy alongside the expert's narrative goals.
pivot.compelling_reason: While the expert's consistency is vital, the researcher's concern matters because it reveals how the expert adapts delivery to fit real-world constraints.
pivot.coarse_scenario: You've highlighted how critical the one-liner's accuracy is, but could you walk me through how you might change that preparation if you were speaking in a noisy hallway versus a quiet conference room?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Editing a headline while writing

structural_mapping.logic: Simultaneous synthesis and collection

analogy.explanation: The researcher expects a clear stop between gathering and summarizing, but like editing a headline while writing, the expert refines the summary continuously as new information arrives rather than waiting until the end.

assistance_payload.scenario: As you scan the chart for the clinical story, do you finish gathering all data before fixing your one-liner, or does the sentence evolve while you read?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Writing a headline for a news story

structural_mapping.inputs: All event facts map to all patient chart data
structural_mapping.logic: Selecting for impact maps to prioritizing by acuity
structural_mapping.outputs: Headline maps to the prioritized problem list

analogy.explanation: Both involve selecting information for an audience, like choosing words for a headline. However, the researcher focuses on individual words (data), while you focus on the headline story (problems), meaning data is only relevant if it supports the story.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you decide which lab values to mention, is it because the number is abnormal, or because it supports the main diagnosis you want the team to focus on first?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Sorting emails by urgency instead of received time

structural_mapping.inputs: Scattered data points (chart notes or inbox messages)
structural_mapping.logic: Filtering for critical importance first rather than sequence
structural_mapping.outputs: Ordered action list (presentation flow or reply priority)

analogy.explanation: Both processes organize scattered inputs into a sequence, but like sorting urgent emails over received time, the expert prioritizes danger over occurrence, showing why a linear timeline fails to capture clinical priority.

assistance_payload.scenario: When a stable patient suddenly crashes, do you start your presentation with the crash instead of walking the team through the timeline leading up to it?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

assistance_payload.validate_focus: The expert emphasizes the critical nature of ordering specific problems within the assessment based on life-threat and learning opportunities. This ensures the audience grasps the most vital clinical decisions first.

pivot.limitation: Focusing solely on problem-level ordering overlooks how the presenter decides to allocate preparation time between the history section and the assessment section overall.
pivot.research_goal: The researcher is trying to understand the macro-structural weighting during the preparation phase, specifically why the assessment might be prioritized over the history in terms of cognitive load.
pivot.compelling_reason: Both levels of prioritization are important; understanding the section-level weighting reveals how experts decide what information is essential enough to include before they even begin ordering specific problems.
pivot.coarse_scenario: That triage of specific problems is vital, and I'm also curious about the bigger picture of how you decide to weight the assessment section against the history section during your prep.

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Giving directions based on transport mode

structural_mapping.logic: Filter by traveler's vehicle (Filter by audience role)

analogy.explanation: Your search for fixed data rules assumes all listeners travel the same road. Like giving directions, the expert knows a route is only 'relevant' if it matches the listener's vehicle. This shows data relevance shifts with the audience, not the chart.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you're filtering a chart before rounds, do you decide what data matters based on the values themselves, or does who you're talking to change what counts as relevant?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

assistance_payload.validate_focus: The expert emphasizes that successful presentations depend on dynamically shifting focus based on the listener's role and expertise level.

pivot.limitation: Focusing solely on audience adaptation misses the foundational cognitive workflow the researcher seeks to map during the preparation phase.
pivot.research_goal: The researcher is trying to understand how the expert initially weights the assessment against the history before contextual adjustments occur.
pivot.compelling_reason: While the expert's contextual strategy is vital, identifying the baseline structural priority helps clarify the magnitude of adjustment required for different audiences.
pivot.coarse_scenario: You've highlighted how crucial audience calibration is, but could you share how you initially decide the weight of the assessment versus the history before you know who you'll be presenting to?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Briefing a boss vs. training a rookie

structural_mapping.logic: Determine Clinical Goal (Decision vs. Reasoning)

analogy.explanation: Both adjust communication, but the researcher focuses on terminology depth like speaking slower for clarity, while the expert shifts the clinical goal like asking a boss for a decision versus teaching a rookie the process.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you present to a senior attending versus a junior intern, do you shift from asking for management decisions to walking through diagnostic reasoning, rather than just changing your vocabulary?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Giving directions to a driver versus a pedestrian

structural_mapping.inputs: Full map data (all streets and paths)
structural_mapping.logic: Filter routes based on traveler capability (wheels vs. feet)

analogy.explanation: The researcher seeks fixed rules for 'relevant' streets, but the analogy shows streets are only relevant based on the traveler's mode, implying the researcher's 'relevance heuristics' are actually the expert's dynamic audience-adaptation rules rather than intrinsic clinical facts.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you round with a pharmacist versus a resident, does the data you label 'irrelevant' stay the same, or does what counts as essential change based on who needs to act on it?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Giving directions to a local versus a tourist

structural_mapping.logic: Adjusting references (street names vs. landmarks) without changing the route
structural_mapping.outputs: Successful arrival via the same path

analogy.explanation: Both concepts involve adapting information for the listener. The analogy clarifies that the expert translates terms (like using landmarks instead of street names) without changing the route, whereas the researcher expects the route itself (clinical details) to change.

assistance_payload.scenario: When presenting to a pharmacist, do you actually remove the pathophysiology details from your story, or do you keep all the facts but just swap out the complex medical terminology?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

assistance_payload.validate_focus: The expert prioritizes how venue and time constraints dynamically dictate the structure of oral presentations. This contextual flexibility is essential for navigating different clinical workflows.

pivot.limitation: Focusing solely on external constraints overlooks the internal cognitive prioritization that occurs during the preparation phase before context is applied.
pivot.research_goal: The researcher aims to understand the inherent weighting of the assessment and plan section against the history section during initial synthesis.
pivot.compelling_reason: Identifying this baseline prioritization reveals core clinical reasoning habits that exist independently of specific workflow demands.
pivot.coarse_scenario: I see how the venue dictates the final length, but could you walk me through how you decide the initial weight of the assessment versus the history during your private prep before those constraints hit?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Talking differently to a child than to a boss

structural_mapping.logic: Automatic depth adjustment based on category, not individual testing

analogy.explanation: Just as you simplify for a child without testing their vocabulary, you treat the venue as a proxy for knowledge, meaning the researcher's 'estimation' happens implicitly through your recognition of the setting.

assistance_payload.scenario: In morning rounds versus grand rounds, do you stop to assess their knowledge level, or does the setting automatically dictate how deep you go?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Telling a story at a bus stop versus around a campfire

structural_mapping.logic: Compress details for speed vs. expand for engagement

analogy.explanation: Both concepts involve adapting content to fit the situation. The researcher thinks you adapt based on who listens (specialty), but you adapt based on where you are (venue). Like shortening a story at a bus stop regardless of the listener, you prioritize brevity on rounds due to workflow constraints.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you present the same case at quick rounds versus a teaching conference, do you cut details because of the clock, or because the specialists listening need different information?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Giving directions when unsure

structural_mapping.inputs: Incomplete route knowledge versus incomplete chart data
structural_mapping.logic: Include uncertain turns with warnings instead of skipping them versus include knowledge gaps with hypotheses instead of hiding them
structural_mapping.outputs: Safe arrival despite gaps versus trustworthy presentation despite uncertainty

analogy.explanation: Just like giving directions when unsure requires mentioning a guessed turn so the driver watches for it, the expert treats uncertainty as relevant data to include with a hypothesis, whereas the researcher's heuristic might filter it out as irrelevant noise.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you spot a gap in the chart, do you treat that uncertainty as relevant data to share with a hypothesis, or do you filter it out until you have confirmed facts?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Admitting you're lost instead of guessing directions

structural_mapping.inputs: Unclear path or missing signpost versus conflicting lab values or gaps in patient history
structural_mapping.logic: Choosing to state uncertainty explicitly versus forcing a connection to maintain forward momentum
structural_mapping.outputs: Safety and trust preserved versus risk of error or false narrative consistency

analogy.explanation: Through the lens of admitting you're lost, the researcher's drive to 'resolve' data looks like guessing a direction to keep the map clean, whereas the expert's honesty looks like stopping to ensure safety, showing they prioritize different outcomes within the same situation.

assistance_payload.scenario: When your data conflicts, do you ever choose to highlight that uncertainty to your attending instead of smoothing it over for a cleaner story?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Choosing which receipts to show during an audit

structural_mapping.logic: Filtering for what supports the plan without inviting challenge

analogy.explanation: Just as an employee defines 'relevant receipts' as those that won't trigger an audit flag rather than every true expense, you define 'relevant data' as what protects your plan from challenge. This shows your relevance heuristic is strategic, not just clinical.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you filter chart data, do you ever omit true findings simply because highlighting them might invite a challenge you'd rather avoid defending?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Standing by a decision when questioned

structural_mapping.logic: Maintain confidence without aggression

analogy.explanation: Both aim for a consistent narrative, but the researcher expects you to resolve data conflicts beforehand like editing a draft, while you focus on standing by a decision when questioned.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you spot conflicting lab results, do you reconcile them before presenting, or do you prepare to stand firm if a senior challenges your interpretation during the rounds?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Holding your ground without starting a fight

structural_mapping.logic: Assess threat level while maintaining your position

analogy.explanation: The researcher views estimating the audience as tuning for understanding. This analogy shows the expert views it as tuning for safety: like holding ground with a boss, you assess the listener to defend your plan without conflict, not just to simplify words.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you gauge what the senior knows, are you adjusting your words for their clarity, or adjusting your stance to defend your plan without conflict?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

assistance_payload.validate_focus: The expert emphasizes the importance of shifting emphasis mid-presentation based on audience cues. This dynamic skill ensures the delivery remains responsive to the clinical training setting.

pivot.limitation: Focusing solely on the expert's dynamic delivery behaviors overlooks the static rules established earlier.
pivot.research_goal: The researcher aims to understand how the assessment section is weighted against the history during the preparation phase.
pivot.compelling_reason: While the expert's delivery skills are vital, the researcher needs to know how preparation constraints enable or limit those real-time adjustments.
pivot.coarse_scenario: I recognize how critical those real-time adjustments are for engagement. However, I also need to understand how you prioritize the assessment over history during prep, since that initial weighting determines what you have available to adjust later.

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

probes.question: What primarily signals a need to adjust narrative depth during the talk?
Which visual cue weighs heavier in your assessment of understanding?
How do you handle mid-flow questions to maintain narrative control?
Which specific signal forces an immediate pivot in your content?
How many interruptions trigger a reduction in technical depth?
What do you sacrifice first when audience attention drops?
What gets cut when time runs short during the session?
Which restlessness cue is most critical for triggering an adjustment?

hypothetical_scenarios.scenario: If your speaking pace changed from moderate to rapid, would your judgment about 'Real-Time Narrative Adjustment' change?
If the audience composition changed from mixed-expertise to homogenous, would your judgment about 'Real-Time Narrative Adjustment' change?
If the Q&A format changed from open-mic to submitted cards, would your judgment about 'Real-Time Narrative Adjustment' change?
If the audience visibility changed from full-face to profile-only, would your judgment about 'Real-Time Narrative Adjustment' change?
If the interruption rate changed from frequent to nonexistent, would your judgment about 'Real-Time Narrative Adjustment' change?
If the slide complexity changed from text-heavy to visual-only, would your judgment about 'Real-Time Narrative Adjustment' change?
If the allotted time changed from 30 minutes to 60 minutes, would your judgment about 'Real-Time Narrative Adjustment' change?
If the seating comfort changed from rigid chairs to soft sofas, would your judgment about 'Real-Time Narrative Adjustment' change?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring the preparation process, presentation strategies, and expert judgment involved in delivering oral case presentations in clinical training settings.

analogy.target_concept: Adjusting walking pace to stay beside a friend

structural_mapping.logic: Speed up or slow down instantly

analogy.explanation: Both concepts aim to adapt content, but like adjusting walking pace based on movement rather than shoe type, the expert adjusts based on engagement cues rather than specialty labels.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you see a specialist's eyes glaze over, do you stick to the details their role expects, or do you immediately shift gears?
